title: scholtzia longipedata subsp. procera
intro: Scholtzia longipedata subsp. procera, commonly known as the East Coast Scholtzia, is a shrub native to eastern Australia, characterized by its fragrant, white flowers and needle-like leaves. This plant has been utilized for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Scholtzia longipedata subsp. procera include its potential to alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Scholtzia longipedata subsp. procera include teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
